summary:
The functional fraction of the human genome has no established upper limit due to pervasive non-coding functions, particularly from pseudogenes which constitute about one-third of the genome and regulate gene expression through RNA molecules. These discoveries demonstrate extensive functional diversity outside traditional coding sequences, supporting the absence of a proportional boundary between functional and non-functional DNA.  

\boxed{B}
